description: Donut is a tool for generating position-independent shellcode from VBScript, JScript, .NET assemblies, EXE / DLL files. The Python package wraps the binary.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/donut && python3 donut.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/donut && python3 donut.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/donut && python3 donut.py --help`. Generate position-independent shellcode from VBS/JS/JAR/EXE/DLL.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the c2 category. Fits when the engagement needs: Generate position-independent shellcode from VBS/JS/JAR/EXE/DLL.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Generate position-independent shellcode from VBS/JS/JAR/EXE/DLL.

name: donut

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
